perturbation.original_text: 2.3. Fees. In each of the first twelve months of this Agreement, the Company shall pay to the Provider $40,000 per month in exchange for the Services (the "Service Fee"). Upon each anniversary of the commencement of this Agreement, the Service Fee shall be increased by a percentage that reflects the actual increases in the Provider's cost of providing the Services, if any. All materials and labor required to provide any Repairs or any other services or other work not specified in Section 2.1(a) hereof requested by the Company shall be billed to the Company, in addition to the Service Fee as adjusted, at a rate equal to the Provider's actual cost plus 10% service/overhead fee.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3. Fees. In each of the first twelve months of this Agreement, the Company shall pay to the Provider a Service Fee. Upon each anniversary of the commencement of this Agreement, the Service Fee may be adjusted. All materials and labor required to provide any Repairs or any other services or other work not specified in Section 2.1(a) hereof requested by the Company shall be billed to the Company, in addition to the Service Fee as adjusted, at a rate to be determined.
perturbation.explanation: The specific amount of $40,000 for the Service Fee has been removed, and the mechanism for annual increase, and the actual rate to be determined have been replaced with ambiguities. This creates uncertainty about the actual payment obligations. The phrase 'Service Fee as adjusted' still exists, even though the original base fee and its increase mechanism has been removed, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the determination of fees
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3. Fees. In each of the first twelve months of this Agreement, the Company shall pay to the Provider a Service Fee. Upon each anniversary of the commencement of this Agreement, the Service Fee may be adjusted. All materials and labor required to provide any Repairs or any other services or other work not specified in Section 2.1(a) hereof requested by the Company shall be billed to the Company, in addition to the Service Fee as adjusted, at a rate to be determined.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the specific Service Fee amount ($40,000) and the method for calculating annual increases. This omission creates ambiguity and contradicts the remaining language 'Service Fee as adjusted' because there's no longer a defined base fee or adjustment mechanism. The contradictory text contains this same exact text, so the change is a direct contradiction because it eliminates information used later on in the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1. Excused Non-Performance. The Provider shall not be liable nor deemed to be in default for any delay or failure in performance under this Agreement resulting, directly or indirectly, from causes beyond the reasonable control of the Provider, provided that the Provider shall refund to the Company a pro rata portion of the Service Fee for any period in excess of two (2) weeks during which the Provider fails to or is unable for any reason to provide the Services or Repairs called for under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1. Excused Non-Performance. The Provider shall not be liable nor deemed to be in default for any delay or failure in performance under this Agreement resulting, directly or indirectly, from causes beyond the reasonable control of the Provider, provided that the Provider shall refund to the Company for any period in excess of two (2) weeks during which the Provider fails to or is unable for any reason to provide the Services or Repairs called for under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the pro rata refund requirement from Provider to Company. Because in the first part of the text it indicates, 'The Provider shall not be liable nor deemed to be in default for any delay or failure in performance', it indicates that the provider is in fault. But the obligation to refund in removed. Which makes this section more contradictory and uncertain, where it affects the liability due to non-performance and how refunds would be handle.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1. Excused Non-Performance. The Provider shall not be liable nor deemed to be in default for any delay or failure in performance under this Agreement resulting, directly or indirectly, from causes beyond the reasonable control of the Provider, provided that the Provider shall refund to the Company for any period in excess of two (2) weeks during which the Provider fails to or is unable for any reason to provide the Services or Repairs called for under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the obligation for the provider to issue a pro rata refund, while the contradicted text (which is the original text) states that the provider *shall* refund a pro rata portion of the service fee. The removal of this refund obligation creates a direct conflict because the original contract explicitly requires a refund under certain circumstances, and the modified contract removes that requirement. The removal of the refund provision directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted section, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
